summary: Add Husky and lint-staged pre-commit hooks
problem_statement: Without automated pre-commit checks, linting and formatting violations slip into the repository and are only caught during review or CI — if at all. A pre-commit gate ensures every commit meets the project's quality baseline before it enters the history.
in_scope: Install Husky and lint-staged as dev dependencies
Configure Husky to run lint-staged on the pre-commit hook
Configure lint-staged to run ESLint and Prettier on staged TypeScript and JavaScript files
Verify the hook triggers correctly on a test commit
out_of_scope: CI/CD pipeline setup — tooling is CI-ready but pipeline configuration is a separate concern
Commit message linting (commitlint) — not in R2 scope
Running tests in the pre-commit hook — test execution is not gated at commit time in R2
requirements: Husky and lint-staged are installed as dev dependencies
A pre-commit hook runs lint-staged on every git commit
lint-staged runs ESLint --fix and Prettier --write on staged .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx files
acceptance_criteria: Committing a file with a lint error or formatting violation triggers the hook and blocks the commit
Committing a clean file passes the hook without errors
Husky hooks are installed via a postinstall or prepare script
risks: Husky init may not run automatically for all contributors if prepare script is missing or skipped
implementation_notes: Use the prepare script in package.json to ensure Husky installs hooks on npm install. lint-staged config can live in package.json or a dedicated .lintstagedrc file.
